title: Add user profile pages for sellers to showcase their collections

requirements.text: Create a user profile page component that displays a seller's information including their name, email (if provided), seller status, and member since date
acceptanceCriteria: Profile page displays user's name prominently
Shows email if the user provided one during registration
Displays seller badge or indicator if user is a seller
Shows account creation date in a readable format
Page is accessible via route like /profile/:userId

requirements.text: Display all active listings created by the profile owner on their profile page in a grid layout similar to the browse page
acceptanceCriteria: Profile page shows a grid of all items listed by that seller
Each item displays using the existing ItemCard component
Only active (non-deleted) listings are shown
Shows empty state message if seller has no listings
Items are clickable and navigate to item detail page

requirements.text: Add backend query function to retrieve a user's public profile information by principal ID including their profile data and all their active listings
acceptanceCriteria: Function accepts a Principal parameter for the target user
Returns user profile data (name, email, isSeller, createdAt)
Returns array of all active listings created by that user
Function is callable by any authenticated user (public profile view)
Returns appropriate error if user profile does not exist

requirements.text: Add navigation links to seller profiles from item cards and item detail pages by making the seller name clickable
acceptanceCriteria: Seller name on ItemCard component links to seller's profile page
Seller info on ItemDetailPage links to seller's profile page
Links use the seller's principal ID in the route
Hover state indicates the name is clickable
Navigation works correctly from all pages

requirements.text: Create a React Query hook for fetching user profile data with their listings and integrate it into the profile page component
acceptanceCriteria: Hook added to useQueries.ts following existing patterns
Handles loading, error, and success states
Profile page shows loading spinner while fetching
Displays error message if profile cannot be loaded
Data refreshes appropriately when navigating between profiles

requirements.text: Add a 'View Profile' link or button in the Layout header navigation when user is authenticated that navigates to their own profile page
acceptanceCriteria: Link appears in header navigation next to existing links
Only visible when user is logged in
Navigates to the authenticated user's profile page
Uses the current user's principal ID in the route
Maintains wizarding theme styling consistent with other nav links

constraints: Do not modify existing authentication system or Internet Identity integration
Keep existing messaging and cart functionality unchanged
Profile pages must be publicly viewable by any authenticated user
nonGoals: Profile editing or settings page (separate from ProfileSetupModal)
